1→# Review: deepmind/663.lean
     2→
     3→## 1. Copyright Header
     4→
     5→- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
     6→- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.
     7→
     8→## 2. Import Statement
     9→
    10→- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
    11→- [x] No other imports are present.
    12→
    13→## 3. Module Docstring
    14→
    15→- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
    16→- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 663`.
    17→- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/663](https://www.erdosproblems.com/663)`.
    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines q(n,k), states the conjectured bound).
    19→- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[BEGL96]` and `[Er97e]`.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos663 ... end Erdos663`.
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos663`).
    25→- [x] `open scoped BigOperators` appears **before** the namespace block.
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `leastNondividingPrime` has a LaTeX docstring explaining what it computes and the edge case.
    30→- [x] Marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which involves classical choice).
    31→- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
    32→- [x] Function name uses `lowerCamelCase` (`leastNondividingPrime`).
    33→- N/A: No local notation used (none needed).
    34→- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).
    35→
    36→## 6. Theorem Statements
    37→
    38→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    39→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_663` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    40→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
    41→- [x] Has a docstring explaining the problem statement and the formulation.
    42→- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[BEGL96][Er97e]`.
    43→
    44→### `answer()` usage
    45→
    46→The problem asks "Is it true that..." — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).
    47→
    48→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
    49→- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (so `answer(True)` would mean "yes").
    50→- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ k`, `∀ ε`, `∃ N₀`, `∀ n`) go **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS — not as theorem parameters.
    51→
    52→**Formalization check:** The product `∏ i ∈ Finset.range k, (n + i + 1)` yields `(n+1)(n+2)···(n+k)`, correctly matching `∏_{1 ≤ i ≤ k}(n+i)`. The ε-N₀ formulation correctly captures the `(1 + o(1)) log n` bound.
    53→
    54→## 7. Category Attribute
    55→
    56→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    57→- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
    58→- [x] Proof body contains `sorry` (consistent with `research open`).
    59→
    60→## 8. AMS Attribute
    61→
    62→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
    63→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11`.
    64→- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry).
    65→- [x] No duplicates.
    66→- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (number theory).
    67→
    68→## 9. Proof Bodies
    69→
    70→- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist).
    71→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    72→- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
    73→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    74→
    75→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    76→
    77→- N/A: No variants present. The docstring mentions a stronger conjecture (bound holding up to k = o(log n)) but it is not formalized as a variant. This is acceptable — it could optionally be added as a variant in the future.
    78→
    79→## 11. Code Quality
    80→
    81→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    82→- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `ε`, `≥`, `∈`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `↔`, `→`, `₀`.
    83→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    84→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    85→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
    86→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    87→
    88→## 12. Build Verification
    89→
    90→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
    91→- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.
    92→
    93→## Summary
    94→
    95→**PASS.** The file is well-structured, correctly formalized, and conforms to all applicable checklist items. The definition of `leastNondividingPrime` via `sInf` is clean, the `answer(sorry) ↔ P` pattern is correctly applied for a yes/no question, and the ε-N₀ encoding of the o(1) bound is mathematically faithful. No issues found.
    96→

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/663.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 663`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/663](https://www.erdosproblems.com/663)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defines q(n,k), states the conjectured bound).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[BEGL96]` and `[Er97e]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos663 ... end Erdos663`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos663`).
- [x] `open scoped BigOperators` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `leastNondividingPrime` has a LaTeX docstring explaining what it computes and the edge case.
- [x] Marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which involves classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in the definition.
- [x] Function name uses `lowerCamelCase` (`leastNondividingPrime`).
- N/A: No local notation used (none needed).
- N/A: No `@[category test]` / `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_663` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring explaining the problem statement and the formulation.
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[BEGL96][Er97e]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks "Is it true that..." — this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`.
- [x] RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (so `answer(True)` would mean "yes").
- [x] Quantifiers (`∀ k`, `∀ ε`, `∃ N₀`, `∀ n`) go **after** `answer(sorry)` on the RHS — not as theorem parameters.

**Formalization check:** The product `∏ i ∈ Finset.range k, (n + i + 1)` yields `(n+1)(n+2)···(n+k)`, correctly matching `∏_{1 ≤ i ≤ k}(n+i)`. The ε-N₀ formulation correctly captures the `(1 + o(1)) log n` bound.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` — a valid category.
- [x] Proof body contains `sorry` (consistent with `research open`).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `11`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single entry).
- [x] No duplicates.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (number theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by` / `sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. The docstring mentions a stronger conjecture (bound holding up to k = o(log n)) but it is not formalized as a variant. This is acceptable — it could optionally be added as a variant in the future.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable: `∀`, `∃`, `ε`, `≥`, `∈`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `↔`, `→`, `₀`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

## Summary

**PASS.** The file is well-structured, correctly formalized, and conforms to all applicable checklist items. The definition of `leastNondividingPrime` via `sInf` is clean, the `answer(sorry) ↔ P` pattern is correctly applied for a yes/no question, and the ε-N₀ encoding of the o(1) bound is mathematically faithful. No issues found.
